title: split_signature_params returns Vec<String> but borrows from input
description: signatures::split_signature_params() takes &str and returns Vec<String>. The function parses comma-separated key=value pairs from the Signature header. The returned strings are always substrings of the input (no transformation other than collecting chars). A Vec<&str> (or Vec<Cow<str>>) would avoid allocating a new String per field and would express the borrow relationship correctly. The function is private so this is a purely internal efficiency and idiom issue — no API surface change needed.
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment. Backslash-escape handling requires owned Strings.
